name: ContextMenu

unions.name: ContextMenu
unions.comment: The Model. Put whatever context you like, which is used to create menu items.

unions.name: Cursor
unions.comment: The shape of cursor during hovering on the menu.

unions.name: Direction
unions.comment: The default direction the menu will be shown at.

unions.name: Item
unions.comment:
The menu item. You can construct it with pipe-friendly functions.

```
ContextMenu.item "Take photos"
  |> ContextMenu.icon FontAwesome.camera Color.green
  |> ContextMenu.disabled True
```

unions.name: Overflow
unions.comment: The strategies how to show the menu when it goes out of window.

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment: Defines the styles of the menu. See [examples](https://github.com/jinjor/elm-contextmenu/blob/master/examples/Configs.elm).
aliases.type: { width : Int, direction : Direction, overflowX : Overflow, overflowY : Overflow, containerColor : Color, hoverColor : Color, invertText : Bool, cursor : Cursor, rounded : Bool, fontFamily : String }

values.name: disabled
values.comment: Disables the item. True = disabled, False = enabled.

values.name: icon
values.comment:
Shows the icon.

The first function creates the icon.
This fuction is compatible with
 [elm-material-icons](http://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-community/elm-material-icons/latest) and
 [elm-font-awesome](http://package.elm-lang.org/packages/jystic/elm-font-awesome/latest).
values.type: (Color -> Int -> Html Never) -> Color -> Item -> Item

values.name: itemWithAnnotation
values.comment: Creates an item with annotation which will displayed just below the item name.

values.name: open
values.comment:
Makes the attribute that triggers to open the menu.
This attribute is passed for each element that needs a menu.

Arguments:

1. function to transform component's message into user's message
2. the context which is used to create items

values.name: openIf
values.comment:
Similar to `open` but only works under particular condition.

This is useful for debugging on browser.
values.type: Bool -> (Msg context -> msg) -> context -> Attribute msg

values.name: shortcut
values.comment: Displays the shortcut key at the right.

values.name: update
values.type: Msg context -> ContextMenu context -> ( ContextMenu context, Cmd (Msg context) )

values.name: view
values.comment:
Shows the menu. This should be called at only one place.

Arguments:

1. the Config
2. function to transform component's message into user's message
3. function to create item groups
4. the Model
values.type: Config -> (Msg context -> msg) -> (context -> List (List ( Item, msg ))) -> ContextMenu context -> Html msg
